expectations.evidence: 'No. Hiding the parameters is treating the symptom. Split the class.'

expectations.text: Reframes: 9 collaborators is SRP smell, not DI ergonomics
expectations.evidence: 'A 9-arg constructor is not a DI-ergonomics problem. It's the compiler telling you ReportGenerator has too many reasons to change — i.e. it's violating SRP.'

expectations.text: Connects parameter count to actor / multi-stakeholder responsibility
expectations.evidence: 'Five repositories + two services in one class almost always means ReportGenerator is doing several jobs stacked on top of each other.' — frames as multi-job but doesn't say 'actor' explicitly

expectations.text: Correct fix: split along actor / responsibility lines
expectations.evidence: Concrete 4-way split (DataLoader / Calculator / Renderer / orchestrator) with explicit dep-count reduction

expectations.text: Hiding the parameters preserves the underlying problem
expectations.evidence: 'ServiceLocator... make[s] the smell quieter without removing it' — explicitly names symptom-suppression
